---
title: "Mitigation"
format: html
---

# 🌿 Mitigation: Reducing Our Footprint 

Goal: To achieve carbon neutrality in all facilities and operations by 2035,
serving as a faithful example of environmental stewardship for our communities. 

-----

## 📉 Our Commitment 

In July 2022, The Episcopal Church’s General Convention committed the entire
church to reaching carbon neutrality by 2030.  While the direct impact of a
single congregation may seem small, our actions stand as vital examples that can
inspire others to join in the work of healing God's creation. 

-----

## 🔍 1. Discovery & Analysis (The Audit Phase) 

Before jumping into major projects like solar panels, it is vital to first
understand your building's current performance.  Simple, low-cost solutions
identified through audits often yield the highest immediate impact. 

### Essential Audits 

  - **Energy Audit:** Perform a professional energy audit to identify where heat
  is escaping and where electrical loads can be reduced. 
  - **Water Audit:** Conduct a full water audit to find hidden leaks and inefficient fixtures. 
  - **Waste Audit:** Identify opportunities to reduce landfill waste through
  composting, recycling, and eliminating single-use plastics. 

-----

## 🟢 2. Accessible Improvements (Under $500) 

These "easy wins" can be implemented quickly and often pay for themselves within
months. 

  - **Lighting Retrofits:** Replace all CFL or incandescent bulbs with LEDs.
  This can lower lighting electricity usage by 40%, and the bulbs last twice as
  long. (Be sure to use [Energy
  Star](https://www.energystar.gov/ia/partners/publications/pubdocs/ENERGY%20STAR%20Lighting%20Brochure_508.pdf?94ed-58b5)
  certified bulbs) 
  - **Landscape Maintenance:** Replace two-stroke, gasoline, and diesel
  landscaping maintenance equipment with electric equipment, or engage
  contractors using electric equipment. 
  - **Leak Detection:** Test all toilets for leaks every 6 months.  A single
  leaking toilet can cost up to $50 per month in wasted water. 
  - **Watering Systems:** Adjust irrigation to only spray where needed and plant
  native trees to reduce the long-term need for watering. 
  - **Sustainable Hospitality:** Encourage parishioners to contribute ceramic
  coffee mugs to replace single-use plastic cups. 
  - **Efficiency Upgrades:** 
    + Replace failed dishwashers with Energy Star models. 
    + Install high-efficiency lavatory faucet aerators (.5 to 1.0 GPM). 
    + Insulate all hot water lines with R-3 or higher insulation. 

-----

## 🟡 3. Moderate Investments (Under $5,000) 

These projects require more planning but offer significant long-term savings. 

  - **Smart Climate Control:** Install smart or programmable thermostats to
  optimize heating and cooling. 
  - **Building Envelope:** Seal or replace leaky ductwork and add ceiling
  insulation where needed. 
  - **Mechanical Upgrades:** Replace aging water heaters with high-efficiency
  heat pump models. 
  - **[Fixture
  Upgrades:](https://19january2017snapshot.epa.gov/www3/watersense/products/toilets.html)**
  Replace standard toilets with low-flow models, upgrade faucets. 
  - **Resilient Landscaping:** Transition to xeriscaping with native plants that
  can survive drought without supplemental watering. 

-----

## 🔴 4. Long-Term Hardening (Over $5,000) 

These "hard" projects represent deep shifts in how your facility interacts with
the environment. 

  - **HVAC Modernization:** Replace end-of-life heating and cooling systems with
  the highest-efficiency equipment available. 
  - **Renewable Energy:** Install a solar array to generate clean power on-site. 
  - **Water Stewardship:** Install rainwater cisterns or graywater systems for
  landscape needs. 
  - **Clean Transportation:** Install EV charging stations to support the
  transition to electric vehicles for staff and parishioners. 

-----

### Resource Links From the Previous Task Force 

  - [Environmental Footprint
  Document](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Z0qLzEvj7cUKxxyJ3ATSoeX4pq3j1nqBDojJ0iHTp8Q/edit?usp=sharing)
  
  - [Mitigation Project
  Grid](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1fVLTtoHHzNdOsxTOL1hWNGP4EMiWZlFcK_ybGEBu5o0)
